title: Tags, Signatures, Verification, and Release Identity · Graph Retrieval
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs tags, signatures, verification, and release identity from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the release reference, signature, and artifact provenance packet. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for release reference, signature, and artifact provenance packet after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish lightweight refs from annotated tag objects, nested tags, peeling, target types, messages, tagger identity, and replacement risk.
Verify supported GPG, SSH, or X.509 signatures while separating cryptographic validity, key identity, trust policy, authorization, and time evidence.
Compare signed commits, tags, merges, and pushes by what is covered, who verifies them, and which server or client policy applies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Retrieve the complete evidence cycle
steps.instruction: Order the Gita Tag Object Peeling evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Gita Tag Object Peeling assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Case G58 is a review in a disposable repository. the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review. The learner must predict verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy, run git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0, explain the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist, test this changed condition: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Gita Tag Object Peeling has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Gita Tag Object Peeling precondition or invariant that the review case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G58 is a review in a disposable repository. the annotated tag is cryptographically valid but the signer still needs authorization review. The learner must predict verification exposes signed payload, fingerprint, algorithm, and validity independently of policy, run git verify-tag --raw v2.4.0, explain the signature is valid and the key fingerprint maps to the current release-role allowlist, test this changed condition: move the tag to an unsigned commit and require identity plus artifact provenance checks to fail, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve the observed tag object, target ID, transparency evidence, and artifact digest.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: An annotated tag points directly to file content and cannot target another tag or non-commit object.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Bind reviewed source, verified tag target, clean reproducible build, artifact digest, attestation, publication record, and rollback ref.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
